Solution

Q4 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The image channel selectivity of superheterodyne receiver depends upon,

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The image rejection should be achieved before IF stage because ones the image frequency enters
into IF amplifier, it becomes impossible to remove it from wanted signal. So, image channel
selectivity depends upon preselector and RF amplifiers only and is achieved by tuning the filter
to attenuate the image frequency. The IF amplifier helps in rejection of adjacent channel frequency
and not image frequency.

Q8 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0

NAT
Correct answer: 0.94
Solution

Q9 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following methods are used for the demodulation of FM signal?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
Balanced slope detector: This is a FM demodulator that uses two tuned LC circuits to convert
frequency variations into amplitude variations. The two tuned circuits are connected in back-toback
fashion to opposite ends of centre-tapped transformer to provide better linearity.
Foster-seeley discriminator: This circuit uses a tuned LC circuit and diodes to produce an output
voltage that is proportional to the frequency deviation of the FM signal.
Ratio Detector: This type of discriminator also uses tuned circuits and diodes, but it’s designed
to suppress amplitude variations in the FM signal, improving its robustness.
PLL: PLLs are feedback systems that track the frequency of the incoming FM signal. The PLL’s
output voltage is proportional to the instantaneous frequency of the FM signal, effectively
demodulating it.
